title: A ledger left open
body: Julius sets the counting-house book in front of you, open to a page of his own corrections. He says nothing about who the errors belonged to before he found them.

choices.text: Go through the figures yourself, line by line
Take his word for it and move on

title: The dyeworks apprentice
body: A cloth merchant offers good money for a rush batch of madder-dyed wool, no questions asked about how the colour is fixed so fast. The usual process wants another week the merchant won't wait for.

choices.text: Take the commission and cut the process short
Refuse — the vats are done properly or not at all

title: Word gets around
body: A wool-stapler who lost money to a dyer's shortcut last year hears you turned down easy coin rather than rush a vat. He brings his custom to the Charetty house instead.

choices.text: Accept the custom

title: Simon's courtesy
body: Simon de St Pol finds you on the Bruges quay and delivers a compliment shaped like an insult, loudly enough for the dockhands to hear.

choices.text: Answer with a joke of your own
Say nothing and let him talk

title: A dockside rumour
body: A stevedore at the London wharves offers, for a few pence, a name and a ship due in from Calais with a hold worth knowing about before the rest of the quay does.

choices.text: Pay for the tip
Walk on

title: The rumour proves true
body: The ship the stevedore named comes in exactly as he said, and the cargo it carries moves the London market before anyone else has heard of it.

choices.text: Take the profit

title: Godscalc's counsel
body: Father Godscalc asks, without preamble, whether a bank's ledger and a man's conscience are kept in the same hand or two different ones.

choices.text: Hear him out
Tell him the ledger comes first

title: News from the Rialto
body: A broker at the Rialto steps, recognising the Charetty mark on your manifest, offers to talk further than the going gossip — for a price, and in private.

choices.text: Press him for more
Note the offer and move on

title: Simon remembers
body: A shipment that should have cleared Calais on schedule is delayed a week by a customs officer who owes Simon de St Pol a favour. Nothing can be proven; nothing needs to be.

choices.text: Absorb the loss and say nothing

title: A season's reckoning
body: The dyeworks is quiet for an evening. Marian asks what the last seven months have actually added up to, beyond the entries in the book.

choices.text: Take stock quietly
Celebrate the takings instead

title: The Geneva account
body: The fairs at Geneva run on paper as much as coin. A local factor offers the Charetty house a standing arrangement — letters and small sums handled on your account whenever a courier passes through, for a fee.

choices.text: Open a standing account with the factor
Rely on passing traffic for now

title: The Milan crossing
body: Past the Alpine passes, Milan trades in silk, armour and short tempers. An armourer's foreman, sizing up the Charetty mark, asks whether the house means to be more than a passing customer.

choices.text: Introduce the house properly, for future business
Keep to trade and move on

title: The pledge
body: A woman past the dyeworks gate offers her wedding ring against a loan for winter grain, and will not meet your eye while she says it. The pawnshop side of the business rarely feels like banking; this is one of those days.

choices.text: Lend her the ring's full worth
Lend at the house's usual rate

title: A letter from the Medici agent
body: A Florentine correspondent, writing on behalf of the Medici bank's local agent, asks after the Charetty house's standing and hints at business to come — if the acquaintance is worth deepening.

choices.text: Answer warmly and open the correspondence
Reply briefly and keep the house's own counsel

title: The Medici agent's favour
body: The correspondence has borne fruit. The agent lets slip, in the way of a man doing a favour rather than a service, that a word from him at Florence will smooth the Charetty house's paper there.

choices.text: Accept the goodwill

title: The ostrich
body: A Portuguese factor, stuck with a live ostrich meant as a wedding gift for a Burgundian noble and no way to deliver it presentably, offers good money to anyone willing to see the bird safely to its destination.

choices.text: Take the commission
Decline — the house has enough to manage

title: Delivered, more or less
body: The ostrich arrives at its destination alive, insulted, and short several tail feathers it left behind at three separate posting inns. The noble is delighted regardless.

choices.text: Collect the fee and say nothing about the feathers

title: A Genoese confidence
body: A Genoese contact, well into his cups but not so far gone he doesn't know what he's saying, describes a concealed holding in the Phocea alum works — the kind of knowledge that is worth money precisely because so few people have it.

choices.text: Note it quietly and say nothing further

secret.name: The Phocea alum interest
secret.description: A Genoese contact's concealed holding in the Phocea alum works — modest value, and no urgency to use it.

choices.text: It isn't the house's business

title: Whispers from Rome
body: A report reaches the house, third-hand and unconfirmed, that new alum deposits have been found near Tolfa, within the Papal states — deposits that could break the Ottoman alum trade's whole value if the rumour holds. It will not stay a rumour long.

choices.text: Weigh the whisper carefully and hold it close

secret.name: The Tolfa alum discovery
secret.description: Word of new alum deposits at Tolfa that could break the Ottoman alum monopoly — large value, but it will become common knowledge soon and be worth nothing to hold.

choices.text: Dismiss it as market talk

title: Jordan de Ribérac takes an interest
body: Simon's father is a colder proposition than his son: precise, patient, and now apparently convinced there's a contract technicality worth suing over. The threat arrives by letter, correctly worded and entirely serious.

choices.text: Fight it through Julius and Gregorio
Settle quietly and be done with it

title: A public rebuke
body: The court finds for the Charetty house, and finds it publicly enough that Jordan de Ribérac's composure slips, once, in front of witnesses who will talk about it for months.

choices.text: Let the judgment stand and say no more

title: A quiet knife
body: The settlement cost less than a court fight would have, but it bought no peace. Simon takes the quiet resolution as proof the house can be pushed, and starts looking for the next place to push.

choices.text: Note it and prepare for the next round

title: First blood
body: It happens on the Bruges quay, in daylight, in front of enough people that neither side can pretend afterward it didn't. Words become something closer to a brawl before it's pulled apart. Nobody is badly hurt. Nobody forgets it either.

choices.text: Answer him in kind
Walk away and let the house's standing speak for itself

title: Astorre's opportunity
body: Astorre brings word of a condotta on offer at Naples: the company of lances, paid a retainer to serve a campaign's length, with bonuses if it goes well. He wants the house's blessing to broker it — and its backing.

choices.text: Negotiate hard through Gregorio
Accept the terms offered and move quickly

title: The company of lances comes home
body: Astorre's contract at Naples runs its course. The retainer is paid out in full, the campaign bonus with it, and the company returns to Bruges a little richer and a little harder than it left.

choices.text: Welcome Astorre's company home

title: A commission from the arsenal
body: The Naples campaign needs guns, and Milan's foundries can supply them — if someone will carry them the length of Italy in time to matter. The commission is straightforward and the road is not.

choices.text: Accept the commission
Decline — too much can go wrong on that road

title: The guns of Naples
body: The shipment makes it — six pieces of Milanese ordnance, unloaded on the Naples quay in time to matter. Word of the delivery reaches Astorre's camp before the ink on the receipt is dry.

choices.text: Hand over the shipment

title: The window closes
body: The season for the commission has passed without the guns arriving. Whatever became of the shipment along the road, the campaign it was meant for no longer needs it, and the arsenal wants its advance back.

choices.text: Absorb the loss

title: The Charetty match
body: Marian puts the question to you herself, plainly, the way she does everything: the widow and the apprentice, the company and its heir apparent, made one household instead of two arrangements. It is the practical course. It is also not only that.

choices.text: Marry her, and mean it
Marry her, as the practical course it plainly is

title: News from the field
body: Felix takes a fall at the lists that should not have killed him, and does anyway, the wound turning bad in the space of a fortnight no physician can explain away. Marian's son, dead at the edge of a war that was never his to fight. Nothing in the house's ledgers accounts for this.

choices.text: Grieve with Marian, and let the house wait
Keep the house moving — grief can be paid out later

title: Simon's patience
body: Silence didn't end it. Simon takes your refusal to rise as its own kind of insult, and starts finding smaller, quieter ways to make the house's business harder than it needs to be.

choices.text: Note it and carry on

title: The partnership is sealed
body: The company that started the year as a dyeworks and a dying widow's gamble ends it as a bank with a partner's name on the door. Every gain this year cost something on the other side of the ledger nobody keeps. Niccolo Rising, such as it was, is finished.

choices.text: Take up the bank's seal as partner
